title: 10 possible side effects of Lycium barbarum (goji berry)

side_effects_list.side_effect_desc: Lycium barbarum causes allergic reactions in some individuals due to its high concentration of allergenic compounds such as polysaccharides and glycoproteins. These compounds can trigger an immune response, leading to symptoms like hives, itching, swelling, and difficulty breathing in people with sensitive skin or respiratory systems. Additionally, the fruit's flavonoids and terpenoids may also contribute to allergic reactions, making Lycium barbarum a potential allergen for some consumers.

Lycium barbarum triggers digestive issues due to its high content of anthocyanins and beta-carotene. These powerful antioxidants can cause stomach upset, diarrhea, or nausea in some individuals. Additionally, the fiber-rich composition of Lycium barbarum can lead to constipation or bowel irregularity in others, potentially disrupting normal digestion processes.

Lycium barbarum leads to dizziness due to its potential interactions with certain medications and its ability to cause a drop in blood pressure. The herb's high levels of antioxidants and flavonoids can also lead to lightheadedness or dizziness, especially when taken in large doses or without proper digestion. Additionally, the stimulating effects of Lycium barbarum on the nervous system may contribute to feelings of dizziness or disorientation in some individuals.

Lycium barbarum results in headaches due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. These compounds can cause an increase in blood pressure and heart rate, leading to headaches in some individuals. Additionally, the stimulatory effects of Lycium barbarum on the nervous system may contribute to headache development. As a result, users may experience frequent or severe headaches, particularly when consuming high doses or using the extract for extended periods.

Lycium barbarum induces insomnia due to its high concentration of alkaloids and glycosides. These compounds can interfere with the body's natural sleep-wake cycle by stimulating the nervous system and increasing alertness. Additionally, Lycium barbarum may also cause increased heart rate and blood pressure, leading to difficulties falling asleep or staying asleep throughout the night.

Lycium barbarum increases heart rate due to its high concentration of adaptogenic compounds like polysaccharides and flavonoids. These bioactive molecules stimulate the body's stress response system, causing an increase in sympathetic nervous activity and subsequently, an elevated heart rate. As a result, individuals taking Lycium barbarum may experience an increased heart rate as their bodies adapt to its effects.

Lycium barbarum stimulates sweating. This is due to its ability to increase blood circulation and metabolism, causing an increased heat production in the body, which leads to excessive sweating. Additionally, the berries contain compounds that can stimulate the sweat glands, further contributing to this side effect. As a result, individuals taking Lycium barbarum may experience more frequent or intense episodes of sweating.

Lycium barbarum reduces blood pressure by exerting a hypotensive effect on the body. This is due to its ability to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow, leading to decreased peripheral resistance and reduced systolic blood pressure. The bioactive compounds present in Lycium barbarum, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, are thought to be responsible for this vasodilatory effect, which may also contribute to its potential benefits in cardiovascular disease prevention.

Lycium barbarum affects kidney function due to its high concentration of saponins and polysaccharides. These compounds can increase urine production, potentially leading to dehydration and strain on the kidneys. Additionally, Lycium barbarum may also interact with certain medications, such as diuretics, that already affect kidney function. As a result, some individuals may experience impaired kidney function or changes in their kidney's ability to filter waste and excess fluids from the blood.

Lycium barbarum interferes with iron absorption by reducing the amount of non-heme iron that is absorbed into the bloodstream. This is due to its high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, which can bind to non-heme iron in food and prevent it from being utilized by the body. As a result, individuals who consume Lycium barbarum regularly may experience iron deficiency or anemia if their dietary iron intake is not sufficient.

intro: Lycium barbarum has some side effects when used improperly, such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions. The causes of these side effects are typically attributed to the herb's high concentration of antioxidants and fiber, which can irritate the digestive system in some individuals. For example, a person who already suffers from irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) may experience worsened symptoms, such as persistent bloating or abdominal pain, after consuming goji berry supplements.
intro_desc: Lycium barbarum has some side effects when used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and increased blood pressure. The causes of these side effects are typically linked to excessive consumption, individual sensitivities, or interactions with medications. For instance, severe gastrointestinal issues can worsen to debilitating diarrhea, abdominal pain, and nutrient deficiencies, impacting daily activities and overall well-being.
